content.method: Spatial correlation analysis linking school/committee-level SNI classification rates to settlement-level Roma population share (using proxy indicators where direct ethnic data is unavailable), combined with case-file review replicating ECtHR-referenced methodology; pursue formal data-sharing agreements to obtain ethnically disaggregated administrative data where legally permissible.
content.question: Are discriminatory misclassification patterns affecting Roma children statistically distinguishable from general over-identification trends, and what is the geographic/demographic overlap between high-SEN-identification zones and majority-Roma catchment areas?
content.why_it_matters: This bears directly on Hungary's outstanding ECtHR obligations and on whether any of the six reform proposals (especially T3, T4, T6) would need explicit desegregation safeguards to avoid perpetuating or masking discriminatory sorting; it is foundational to evaluating T6's coordination logic.
